children.heading: Performance report
children.label: a Performance report
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 2024, not later than 120 days after the end of each fiscal year during which fees are collected under this subpart, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report concerning the progress of the Food and Drug Administration in achieving the goals identified in the letters described in section 101(b) of the Animal Drug User Fee Amendments of 2023 toward expediting the animal drug development process and the review of the new and supplemental animal drug applications and investigational animal drug submissions during such fiscal year, the future plans of the Food and Drug Administration for meeting the goals, the review times for abbreviated new animal drug applications, and the administrative procedures adopted by the Food and Drug Administration to ensure that review times for abbreviated new animal drug applications are not increased from their current level due to activities under the user fee program.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Fiscal report
children.label: b Fiscal report
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 2024, not later than 120 days after the end of each fiscal year during which fees are collected under this subpart, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report on the implementation of the authority for such fees during such fiscal year and the use, by the Food and Drug Administration, of the fees collected during such fiscal year for which the report is made.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: c Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make the reports required under subsections (a) and (b) available to the public on the Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives;

children.label: C
children.text: scientific and academic experts;

children.label: D
children.text: veterinary professionals;

children.label: E
children.text: representatives of patient and consumer advocacy groups; and

children.label: F
children.text: the regulated industry.

children.label: A
children.text: publish a notice in the Federal Register requesting public input on the reauthorization;

children.label: B
children.text: hold a public meeting at which the public may present its views on the reauthorization, including specific suggestions for changes to the goals referred to in subsection (a);

children.label: C
children.text: provide a period of 30 days after the public meeting to obtain written comments from the public suggesting changes to this subpart; and

children.label: D
children.text: publish the comments on the Food and Drug Administration’s Internet Web site.

children.heading: Periodic consultation
children.label: 3 Periodic consultation
children.text: Not less frequently than once every 4 months during negotiations with the regulated industry, the Secretary shall hold discussions with representatives of veterinary, patient, and consumer advocacy groups to continue discussions of their views on the reauthorization and their suggestions for changes to this subpart as expressed under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: present the recommendations developed under paragraph (1) to the Congressional committees specified in such paragraph;

children.label: B
children.text: publish such recommendations in the Federal Register;

children.label: C
children.text: provide for a period of 30 days for the public to provide written comments on such recommendations;

children.label: D
children.text: hold a meeting at which the public may present its views on such recommendations; and

children.label: E
children.text: after consideration of such public views and comments, revise such recommendations as necessary.

children.heading: Transmittal of recommendations
children.label: 5 Transmittal of recommendations
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall transmit to Congress the revised recommendations under paragraph (4) a summary of the views and comments received under such paragraph, and any changes made to the recommendations in response to such views and comments.January 15, 20282So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.2

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: A Public availability
children.text: Before presenting the recommendations developed under paragraphs (1) through (5) to Congress, the Secretary shall make publicly available, on the Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration, minutes of all negotiation meetings conducted under this subsection between the Food and Drug Administration and the regulated industry.

children.heading: Content
children.label: B Content
children.text: The minutes described under subparagraph (A) shall summarize any substantive proposal made by any party to the negotiations as well as significant controversies or differences of opinion during the negotiations and their resolution.
